meta.reasoning: J3 suited (3h Jh) is too weak to play from UTG (first to act) in a 4-player game. Folding to preserve chips for stronger spots.
Pocket nines is a strong pre-flop hand. On the button with only two remaining opponents (s4 folded). Raising 3x BB to build the pot, gain information, and apply pressure to the blinds.

meta.reasoning_summary: Holding 4h-2d (small blind) facing a raise to 60. This is one of the worst starting hands in poker — folding is correct.
Ts Qc in BB with pot odds ~2.25:1 (call 40 into 90 pot). s1 raised to 60, s2 and s4 folded. T-Q offsuit has decent showdown potential — calling is correct.

result.summary: s4 wins by chip lead after 30 hands (s4: 1370, s3: 1060, s1: 940, s2: 630). Biggest pot: 450. Showdowns: 0.
